heroHeadline: Trump Directs DOJ to Prioritize Death Penalty and Authorize Firing Squads; DOJ Drops Powell Probe
heroSubtitle: In a sweeping criminal justice directive on April 24, the Trump administration ordered the DOJ to prioritize the death penalty in eligible cases and authorized firing squads as an execution method. Separately, the DOJ dropped its probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell — a move that could ease Senate confirmation of Kevin Warsh as Trump's preferred successor. Earlier on April 24, Trump also threatened new tariffs on the UK over its digital services tax, broadening the trade war to a key ally.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, NPR, Bloomberg, CNBC, Axios, NBC News, CBS News, Fox News, Gulf News, The National, Newsweek, Nate Silver/Silver Bulletin, YouGov/Economist, Fox News, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model, IMF.
